term: DET (Dubai Department of Economy and Tourism)
plain_definition: Dubai's general economic licensing and consumer-protection department -- not a dedicated virtual-asset regulator itself, but a body VARA works with on shared administrative functions such as business licensing, inspections, enforcement support, and consumer complaints for virtual asset businesses operating in Dubai.
formal_definition: DET is the Dubai Government department responsible for commercial licensing, business registration, and consumer protection (via its Dubai Corporation for Consumer Protection & Fair Trade unit) across the Emirate of Dubai. Under a Memorandum of Understanding announced by VARA on 10 August 2023, DET incorporated VARA's virtual-asset licensing activities into its own licensing system, undertakes on-site inspections and enforcement support (including suspensions or revocations) on VARA's behalf, and gave VARA access to its E-Permit system for approvals related to virtual-asset events; the two bodies also agreed to coordinate consumer-protection campaigns and jointly publish penalty notices and advisories.
